blurb: At runtime, Apple operating systems enforce multiple layers of OS integrity protection. Kernel Integrity Protection (KIP) on Apple silicon prevents unauthorized modification of kernel code and data at runtime. Code signing is required for all executable code, with AMFI verifying developer signatures and Apple-issued entitlements before allowing code to execute. Address Space Layout Randomization (ASLR), non-executable memory pages, and Pointer Authentication Codes (PAC) defend against memory corruption exploitation. On macOS, System Integrity Protection restricts root-level modification of protected system directories, maintaining OS integrity even if an attacker obtains administrative privileges.
